speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Welcome, sweet prince, to London, to your chamber.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Welcome, dear cousin, my thoughts’ sovereign.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The weary way hath made you melancholy.

speaker: PRINCE
text: No, uncle, but our crosses on the way

speaker: PRINCE
text: Have made it tedious, wearisome, and heavy.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I want more uncles here to welcome me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sweet prince, the untainted virtue of your years

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hath not yet dived into the world’s deceit;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nor more can you distinguish of a man

speaker: RICHARD
text: Than of his outward show, which, God He knows,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Seldom or never jumpeth with the heart.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Those uncles which you want were dangerous.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your Grace attended to their sugared words

speaker: RICHARD
text: But looked not on the poison of their hearts.

speaker: RICHARD
text: God keep you from them, and from such false friends.

speaker: PRINCE
text: God keep me from false friends, but they were none.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My lord, the Mayor of London comes to greet you.

speaker: MAYOR
text: God bless your Grace with health and happy days.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I thank you, good my lord, and thank you all.—

speaker: PRINCE
text: I thought my mother and my brother York

speaker: PRINCE
text: Would long ere this have met us on the way.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Fie, what a slug is Hastings that he comes not

speaker: PRINCE
text: To tell us whether they will come or no!

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And in good time here comes the sweating lord.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Welcome, my lord. What, will our mother come?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: On what occasion God He knows, not I,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: The Queen your mother and your brother York

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Have taken sanctuary. The tender prince

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Would fain have come with me to meet your Grace,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But by his mother was perforce withheld.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Fie, what an indirect and peevish course

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Is this of hers!—Lord Cardinal, will your Grace

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Persuade the Queen to send the Duke of York

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Unto his princely brother presently?—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: If she deny, Lord Hastings, go with him,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And from her jealous arms pluck him perforce.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: My lord of Buckingham, if my weak oratory

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Can from his mother win the Duke of York,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Anon expect him here; but if she be obdurate

speaker: CARDINAL
text: To mild entreaties, God in heaven forbid

speaker: CARDINAL
text: We should infringe the holy privilege

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Of blessèd sanctuary! Not for all this land

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Would I be guilty of so deep a sin.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: You are too senseless obstinate, my lord,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Too ceremonious and traditional.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Weigh it but with the grossness of this age,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: You break not sanctuary in seizing him.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The benefit thereof is always granted

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To those whose dealings have deserved the place

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And those who have the wit to claim the place.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This prince hath neither claimed it nor deserved it

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And therefore, in mine opinion, cannot have it.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Then taking him from thence that is not there,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: You break no privilege nor charter there.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Oft have I heard of sanctuary men,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But sanctuary children, never till now.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: My lord, you shall o’errule my mind for once.—

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Come on, Lord Hastings, will you go with me?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I go, my lord.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Good lords, make all the speedy haste you may.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Say, uncle Gloucester, if our brother come,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Where shall we sojourn till our coronation?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where it seems best unto your royal self.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If I may counsel you, some day or two

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your Highness shall repose you at the Tower;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then where you please and shall be thought most fit

speaker: RICHARD
text: For your best health and recreation.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I do not like the Tower, of any place.—

speaker: PRINCE
text: Did Julius Caesar build that place, my lord?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He did, my gracious lord, begin that place,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which, since, succeeding ages have re-edified.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Is it upon record, or else reported

speaker: PRINCE
text: Successively from age to age, he built it?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Upon record, my gracious lord.

speaker: PRINCE
text: But say, my lord, it were not registered,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Methinks the truth should live from age to age,

speaker: PRINCE
text: As ’twere retailed to all posterity,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Even to the general all-ending day.

speaker: RICHARD
text: So wise so young, they say, do never live long.

speaker: PRINCE
text: What say you, uncle?

speaker: RICHARD
text: I say, without characters fame lives long.

speaker: RICHARD
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              Thus, like the formal Vice, Iniquity,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I moralize two meanings in one word.

speaker: PRINCE
text: That Julius Caesar was a famous man.

speaker: PRINCE
text: With what his valor did enrich his wit,

speaker: PRINCE
text: His wit set down to make his valor live.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Death makes no conquest of this conqueror,

speaker: PRINCE
text: For now he lives in fame, though not in life.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I’ll tell you what, my cousin Buckingham—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: What, my gracious lord?

speaker: PRINCE
text: An if I live until I be a man,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I’ll win our ancient right in France again

speaker: PRINCE
text: Or die a soldier, as I lived a king.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Short summers lightly have a forward spring.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Now in good time here comes the Duke of York.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Richard of York, how fares our loving brother?

speaker: YORK
text: Well, my dread lord—so must I call you now.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Ay, brother, to our grief, as it is yours.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Too late he died that might have kept that title,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which by his death hath lost much majesty.

speaker: RICHARD
text: How fares our cousin, noble lord of York?

speaker: YORK
text: I thank you, gentle uncle. O my lord,

speaker: YORK
text: You said that idle weeds are fast in growth.

speaker: YORK
text: The Prince my brother hath outgrown me far.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He hath, my lord.

speaker: YORK
text: And therefore is he idle?

speaker: RICHARD
text: O my fair cousin, I must not say so.

speaker: YORK
text: Then he is more beholding to you than I.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He may command me as my sovereign,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But you have power in me as in a kinsman.

speaker: YORK
text: I pray you, uncle, give me this dagger.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My dagger, little cousin? With all my heart.

speaker: PRINCE
text: A beggar, brother?

speaker: YORK
text: Of my kind uncle, that I know will give,

speaker: YORK
text: And being but a toy, which is no grief to give.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A greater gift than that I’ll give my cousin.

speaker: YORK
text: A greater gift? O, that’s the sword to it.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, gentle cousin, were it light enough.

speaker: YORK
text: O, then I see you will part but with light gifts.

speaker: YORK
text: In weightier things you’ll say a beggar nay.

speaker: RICHARD
text: It is too heavy for your Grace to wear.

speaker: YORK
text: I weigh it lightly, were it heavier.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What, would you have my weapon, little lord?

speaker: YORK
text: I would, that I might thank you as you call me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: How?

speaker: YORK
text: Little.

speaker: PRINCE
text: My lord of York will still be cross in talk.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Uncle, your Grace knows how to bear with him.

speaker: YORK
text: You mean, to bear me, not to bear with me.—

speaker: YORK
text: Uncle, my brother mocks both you and me.

speaker: YORK
text: Because that I am little, like an ape,

speaker: YORK
text: He thinks that you should bear me on your shoulders.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: With what a sharp-provided wit he reasons!

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To mitigate the scorn he gives his uncle,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He prettily and aptly taunts himself.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: So cunning and so young is wonderful.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My lord, will ’t please you pass along?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Myself and my good cousin Buckingham

speaker: RICHARD
text: Will to your mother, to entreat of her

speaker: RICHARD
text: To meet you at the Tower and welcome you.

speaker: YORK
text: What, will you go unto the Tower, my lord?

speaker: PRINCE
text: My Lord Protector needs will have it so.

speaker: YORK
text: I shall not sleep in quiet at the Tower.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, what should you fear?

speaker: YORK
text: Marry, my uncle Clarence’ angry ghost.

speaker: YORK
text: My grandam told me he was murdered there.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I fear no uncles dead.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nor none that live, I hope.

speaker: PRINCE
text: An if they live, I hope I need not fear.

speaker: PRINCE
text:
To
                 
                York
                .
                 
              But come, my lord. With a heavy heart,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Thinking on them, go I unto the Tower.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Think you, my lord, this little prating York

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Was not incensèd by his subtle mother

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To taunt and scorn you thus opprobriously?

speaker: RICHARD
text: No doubt, no doubt. O, ’tis a parlous boy,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Bold, quick, ingenious, forward, capable.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He is all the mother’s, from the top to toe.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Well, let them rest.—Come hither, Catesby.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Thou art sworn as deeply to effect what we intend

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As closely to conceal what we impart.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Thou knowest our reasons, urged upon the way.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: What thinkest thou? Is it not an easy matter

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To make William Lord Hastings of our mind

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For the installment of this noble duke

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: In the seat royal of this famous isle?

speaker: CATESBY
text: He, for his father’s sake, so loves the Prince

speaker: CATESBY
text: That he will not be won to aught against him.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: What think’st thou then of Stanley? Will not he?

speaker: CATESBY
text: He will do all in all as Hastings doth.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Well then, no more but this: go, gentle Catesby,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And, as it were far off, sound thou Lord Hastings

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: How he doth stand affected to our purpose

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And summon him tomorrow to the Tower

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To sit about the coronation.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: If thou dost find him tractable to us,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Encourage him and tell him all our reasons.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: If he be leaden, icy, cold, unwilling,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Be thou so too, and so break off the talk,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And give us notice of his inclination;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For we tomorrow hold divided councils,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Wherein thyself shalt highly be employed.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Commend me to Lord William. Tell him, Catesby,

speaker: RICHARD
text: His ancient knot of dangerous adversaries

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tomorrow are let blood at Pomfret Castle,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And bid my lord, for joy of this good news,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Give Mistress Shore one gentle kiss the more.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Good Catesby, go effect this business soundly.

speaker: CATESBY
text: My good lords both, with all the heed I can.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall we hear from you, Catesby, ere we sleep?

speaker: CATESBY
text: You shall, my lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: At Crosby House, there shall you find us both.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Now, my lord, what shall we do if we perceive

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Lord Hastings will not yield to our complots?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Chop off his head. Something we will determine.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And look when I am king, claim thou of me

speaker: RICHARD
text: The earldom of Hereford, and all the movables

speaker: RICHARD
text: Whereof the King my brother was possessed.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I’ll claim that promise at your Grace’s hand.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And look to have it yielded with all kindness.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Come, let us sup betimes, that afterwards

speaker: RICHARD
text: We may digest our complots in some form.
